other_observers: Doug Faulkner, Cole Wild, and Richard Stevens were present while I was there.; I believe Jim Schmoker had first found the bird that morning.
specific_location: At the Barr Lake State Park Nature Center.
description: The bird was a smallish flycatcher with a somewhat peaked crown and short bill. The crown, throat, sides of the neck, and all underparts were a brilliant scarlet red. The bill was black. There was a black mask through the eye and rear face. The nape, upperparts and tail were black, with a bit of white fringing along the coverts. The legs were black.Behaviors: The bird would perch at the end of tree branches, then sally out after moths and then land at the tip of another tree branch.Call: At one time it gave a soft 'pit pit pit, pedyear'.Plumage: Alternate
